mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55% of instances where UNRATE increases by more than 0.2pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and stable consumer price inflation indicates robust consumer demand without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive RSAFS growth and CPIAUCSL stability yields negative 60-day returns in greater than 60% of observed periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects immediate capacity utilization and corporate output expansion, generating positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in greater than 50% of periods following positive INDPRO month-over-month changes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 5 percent signals a sharp contraction in residential construction activity and downstream consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in greater than 65% of instances when HOUST drops by more than 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm broad-based labor market strength and sustained wage growth, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE produces negative 60-day returns in greater than 55% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: An unexpected federal funds rate hike exceeding 25 basis points triggers immediate portfolio rebalancing away from equities toward fixed income, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases > 0.25pp in a single FOMC meeting
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in greater than 50% of instances following rate hikes exceeding 0.25pp.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding aggregate economic output and corporate revenue generation, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show negative mean when GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth is positive over a 5-year rolling window.
